news_summary: Today's technology news highlights groundbreaking advancements in ultra-fast photodetection, innovative air traffic management and advanced mobility projects, and the discovery of a uniquely shaped Möbius strip-like molecule.
news_summary_fr: L'actualité technologique d'aujourd'hui met en avant des avancées révolutionnaires dans le domaine de la photodétection ultra-rapide, des projets innovants en matière de gestion du trafic aérien et de mobilité avancée, ainsi que la découverte d'une molécule unique en forme de ruban de Möbius.
news_summary_es: Las noticias tecnológicas de hoy destacan avances revolucionarios en fotodetección ultrarrápida, gestión innovadora del tráfico aéreo y proyectos avanzados de movilidad, así como el descubrimiento de una molécula con una forma única similar a una cinta de Möbius.

articles.title: Record-breaking photodetector captures light in just 125 picoseconds
articles.summary: A new ultrathin photodetector from Duke University can sense light across the entire electromagnetic spectrum and generate a signal in just 125 picoseconds, making it the fastest pyroelectric detector ever built. The breakthrough could power next-generation multispectral cameras used in medicine, agriculture, and space-based sensing.
articles.content:
Electrical engineers at Duke University have created the fastest pyroelectric photodetector ever demonstrated, a device that detects light by sensing the tiny amount of heat it produces when absorbed.

The ultrathin sensor can capture light across the entire electromagnetic spectrum. It operates at room temperature, requires no external power source, and can be integrated directly into on-chip systems. The technology could eventually enable a new generation of multispectral cameras with applications in areas such as skin cancer detection, food safety monitoring, and large scale agriculture.

The findings were reported in the journal Advanced Functional Materials.

Why Traditional Photodetectors Have Limits

Most digital cameras rely on semiconductor photodetectors that produce an electrical current when struck by visible light. Computers then convert that signal into the images we see.

However, semiconductors can detect only a small portion of the electromagnetic spectrum. In that sense, they are similar to the human eye, which is also limited to visible wavelengths of light.

To detect light outside that range, researchers often turn to pyroelectric detectors. These devices produce an electrical signal when they warm up after absorbing incoming light. But generating enough heat from harder to capture wavelengths has traditionally required thick absorbing materials or very bright illumination, making such detectors bulky and slow.

"Commercial pyroelectric detectors aren't very responsive, so they need a very bright light or very thick absorbers to work, which naturally makes them slow because heat doesn't move that fast," said Maiken Mikkelsen, professor of electrical and computer engineering at Duke. "Our approach cleverly integrates near-perfect absorbers and super-thin pyroelectrics to achieve a response time of 125 picoseconds, which is a huge improvement for the field."

Metasurface Design Traps Light Efficiently

The device developed by Mikkelsen's lab relies on a specially engineered structure known as a metasurface. It consists of precisely arranged silver nanocubes positioned on a transparent layer located just 10 nanometers above a thin sheet of gold.

When light hits a nanocube, it excites electrons in the silver. This interaction traps the light's energy through a process called plasmonics. The exact frequency of light captured depends on the size of the nanocubes and the spacing between them.

Because this light trapping is extremely efficient, only a very thin layer of pyroelectric material is needed underneath the structure to generate an electrical signal. Mikkelsen's team first demonstrated the concept in 2019, although the original setup was not designed to measure how quickly the device could respond.

"Thermal photodetectors are supposed to be slow, so this was mind-boggling to the entire community," Mikkelsen said. "We were taken off guard that it seemed to be working on time scales similar to that of silicon photodetectors."

Optimizing the Device for Speed

Over the past several years, Eunso Shin, a PhD student in Mikkelsen's laboratory, has worked to refine the design while also developing a method to measure the device's speed without relying on extremely expensive equipment.

In the newest version of the detector, the metasurface that absorbs light was redesigned into a circular shape rather than a rectangular one. This configuration increases the surface area exposed to incoming light while reducing the distance electrical signals must travel. The researchers also incorporated even thinner pyroelectric layers supplied by collaborators and improved the electronic circuitry used to capture and transmit the signals.

To measure the detector's performance, Shin devised an experimental setup using two distributed feedback lasers. The lasers intensified when their frequencies approached the operating speed of the device, allowing the researchers to determine how quickly the detector could respond.

Their measurements showed that the thermal photodetector can operate at speeds up to 2.8 GHz. At that rate, incoming light produces an electrical signal in only 125 picoseconds.

"Pyroelectric photodetectors commonly operate in the nano-to-microsecond range, so this is hundreds or thousands of times faster," Shin said. "These results are really exciting, but we're still working to make them even faster while figuring out the kinetic limit of pyroelectric photodetectors."

Future Applications From Agriculture to Medicine

The researchers believe the device could become even faster by placing the pyroelectric material and electronic readout components in the narrow gap between the nanocubes and the gold layer. They are also exploring ways to expand the system's capabilities, including designs that use multiple metasurfaces to detect several wavelengths of light and their polarity at the same time.

As development continues and manufacturing challenges are addressed, the technology could open the door to powerful new imaging systems. Because the detectors do not need external power, they could be deployed in drones, satellites, and spacecraft.

Such systems could support precision agriculture by revealing in real time which crops require additional water or fertilizer.

"When you get into the ability to detect lots of frequencies at once, you open the door to so many different things," Mikkelsen said. "Cancer diagnosis, food safety, remote sensing vehicles. Those are all still pretty far down the line, but that's the direction we're heading in."

This research was supported by the Air Force Office of Scientific Research (FA9550-21-1-0312) and the Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation (GBMF8804).
articles.reasoning: The article describes a significant technological breakthrough in photodetector speed and efficiency with broad potential applications in medicine, agriculture, and space sensing. The ultrathin photodetector operates across the entire electromagnetic spectrum, is energy efficient, and could enable next-generation multispectral imaging systems with meaningful real-world benefits for public health, food safety, and environmental monitoring. The story is focused, detailed, and highlights a tangible advancement with wide societal impact.
articles.personality_title: Duke University creates fastest light sensor working in just 125 picoseconds
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Traditional photodetectors in cameras only capture visible light and can be slow or bulky when detecting other types of light. Pyroelectric detectors, which sense light by the heat it produces, have been limited by slow response times and large sizes.

**What happened** – Engineers at Duke University built a new ultrathin pyroelectric photodetector that detects light across the entire electromagnetic spectrum and generates a signal in just 125 picoseconds (one trillionth of a second). This device uses a special metasurface made of tiny silver cubes and gold layers to trap light very efficiently. It works at room temperature without needing extra power and can be made very small.

**Impact** – This photodetector is hundreds to thousands of times faster than previous pyroelectric detectors. Because it can sense many types of light quickly and without extra power, it could lead to advanced cameras for detecting skin cancer, checking food safety, and monitoring crops from drones or satellites. Its speed and efficiency are breakthroughs for future imaging technologies.

**What's next step** – Researchers plan to make the device even faster and able to detect multiple light wavelengths at once. They are working on improving the design and manufacturing so it can be used in real-world applications like medicine, agriculture, and space exploration.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Duke’s new ultrathin photodetector is the fastest of its kind, opening the way for powerful, energy-efficient cameras that see light far beyond what normal cameras can detect.
articles.personality_title_fr: L'Université Duke crée le capteur de lumière le plus rapide fonctionnant en seulement 125 picosecondes
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les photodétecteurs traditionnels dans les caméras ne captent que la lumière visible et peuvent être lents ou encombrants pour détecter d'autres types de lumière. Les détecteurs pyroélectriques, qui détectent la lumière grâce à la chaleur qu'elle produit, sont limités par leur lenteur et leur taille.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Des ingénieurs de l'Université Duke ont construit un nouveau photodétecteur pyroélectrique ultrafin capable de détecter la lumière sur tout le spectre électromagnétique et de générer un signal en seulement 125 picosecondes (un milliardième de milliardième de seconde). Ce dispositif utilise une métasurface spéciale composée de minuscules cubes d'argent et de couches d'or pour piéger la lumière très efficacement. Il fonctionne à température ambiante sans source d'énergie externe et peut être très petit.

**Impact** – Ce photodétecteur est des centaines à des milliers de fois plus rapide que les détecteurs pyroélectriques précédents. Grâce à sa capacité à détecter rapidement de nombreux types de lumière sans énergie supplémentaire, il pourrait permettre des caméras avancées pour détecter le cancer de la peau, contrôler la sécurité alimentaire et surveiller les cultures depuis des drones ou des satellites. Sa rapidité et son efficacité représentent une avancée majeure pour les technologies d'imagerie.

**Prochaine étape** – Les chercheurs prévoient de rendre l'appareil encore plus rapide et capable de détecter plusieurs longueurs d'onde de lumière en même temps. Ils travaillent à améliorer la conception et la fabrication pour une utilisation concrète en médecine, agriculture et exploration spatiale.

**Conclusion en une phrase** – Le nouveau photodétecteur ultrafin de Duke est le plus rapide de son genre, ouvrant la voie à des caméras puissantes et économes en énergie capables de voir bien au-delà de ce que les caméras normales peuvent détecter.
articles.personality_title_es: La Universidad Duke crea el sensor de luz más rápido que funciona en solo 125 picosegundos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los fotodetectores tradicionales en cámaras solo capturan luz visible y pueden ser lentos o voluminosos para detectar otros tipos de luz. Los detectores piroeléctricos, que detectan la luz por el calor que produce, han estado limitados por su lentitud y tamaño.

**Qué pasó** – Ingenieros de la Universidad Duke crearon un nuevo fotodetector piroeléctrico ultradelgado que detecta luz en todo el espectro electromagnético y genera una señal en solo 125 picosegundos (una billonésima de segundo). Este dispositivo usa una metasuperficie especial hecha de pequeños cubos de plata y capas de oro para atrapar la luz de forma muy eficiente. Funciona a temperatura ambiente sin necesidad de energía externa y puede ser muy pequeño.

**Impacto** – Este fotodetector es cientos o miles de veces más rápido que los detectores piroeléctricos anteriores. Debido a que puede detectar muchos tipos de luz rápidamente y sin energía extra, podría permitir cámaras avanzadas para detectar cáncer de piel, controlar la seguridad alimentaria y monitorear cultivos desde drones o satélites. Su velocidad y eficiencia son avances importantes para futuras tecnologías de imagen.

**Próximo paso** – Los investigadores planean hacer el dispositivo aún más rápido y capaz de detectar múltiples longitudes de onda de luz al mismo tiempo. Trabajan en mejorar el diseño y la fabricación para que se use en aplicaciones reales como medicina, agricultura y exploración espacial.

**Conclusión en una frase** – El nuevo fotodetector ultradelgado de Duke es el más rápido de su tipo, abriendo el camino para cámaras potentes y eficientes que pueden ver mucho más allá de lo que detectan las cámaras normales.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of a delicate, glowing silver nanocube lattice hovering just above a thin golden sheet, with subtle waves of light energy flowing swiftly through the structure, symbolizing ultrafast light capture and heat sensing, set against a soft gradient background of natural earth tones.

articles.title: About Air Traffic Management and Safety Project
articles.summary: The Air Traffic Management and Safety (ATMS) project defines, validates, and transfers advanced requirements and technologies to shift air traffic management from tactical to strategic.  This change enables efficient, productive, and resilient operations while reducing safety assurance and compliance costs for highly automated systems.   ATMS researches and develops technologies that safely integrate new air vehicles with traditional aviation operations to meet growing demand. Through close collaboration with the FAA, ATMS delivers actionable automation solutions, advanced operational concepts, […]
articles.content:
Computer simulation showing how aircraft and other vehicles of all types can safely navigate through the National Air Space. NASA / Kyle Jenkins

The Air Traffic Management and Safety (ATMS) project defines, validates, and transfers advanced requirements and technologies to shift air traffic management from tactical to strategic.

This change enables efficient, productive, and resilient operations while reducing safety assurance and compliance costs for highly automated systems.

ATMS researches and develops technologies that safely integrate new air vehicles with traditional aviation operations to meet growing demand. Through close collaboration with the FAA, ATMS delivers actionable automation solutions, advanced operational concepts, and proactive safety management frameworks that accelerate airspace modernization.

ATMS strengthens system resilience and expands human capacity by reducing cognitive workload, minimizing airline delays, and lowering operating costs while enhancing terminal safety and optimizing operational performance.

ATMS tackles barriers in the increasingly complex and diverse airspace by focusing its research on three areas:

Strategic Harmonization for Integrated Flows and Trajectories

The National Airspace System (NAS) is evolving toward greater complexity and demand. Current tactical approaches limit scalability, efficiency, and predictability. ATMS research represents a paradigm change—from reactive, tactical decision-making to proactive, strategic management of traffic flows and trajectories.

Safely Enable Routine Autonomous Operations

Advancements in automation can reduce human workload, mitigate hazards, and enable new entrants across advanced air mobility. Critical gaps—in hazard perception and avoidance, seamless ATC integration, and flight procedures—still pose safety and operational risks. Without ATMS’ targeted research, autonomous taxi, approach, and landing will remain fragmented and heavily human-dependent, limiting efficiency and innovation.

Assurance Methods for Aircraft Automation

The aviation community is converging on assurance approaches that balance trust, evidence, and scalability. To ensure innovation and adoption of key automation capabilities, ATMS helps to define explicit safety objectives and meaningful notions of traceability across development and operations. Scaled adoption requires assurance processes that integrate design and operational assurance, so that requirements flow down to models, scenarios, analysis, test cases and metrics—and that these generate traceable, reusable evidence and operational outcomes.

ATMS delivers practical solutions that benefit every stakeholder in the aviation ecosystem—from air traffic controllers and pilots to passengers and operators—ensuring America ‘s skies remain the safest and most efficient in the world.
articles.reasoning: The article describes a significant technological advancement in air traffic management that improves safety, efficiency, and operational resilience in the national airspace system. It highlights broad benefits for the general public, including safer skies, reduced delays, and support for autonomous operations, with detailed context on the project's goals and impact.
articles.personality_title: New Air Traffic Project Aims to Make Flying Safer and Smoother
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – The National Airspace System (NAS) is becoming busier and more complex as more types of aircraft take to the skies. Managing all this air traffic safely and efficiently is a big challenge. 

**What happened** – The Air Traffic Management and Safety (ATMS) project is developing new technologies and rules to change how air traffic is controlled. Instead of reacting to problems as they happen (tactical), ATMS focuses on planning ahead (strategic) to manage flights better. It works closely with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to create automation tools and safety systems that help both traditional airplanes and new types of air vehicles fly safely together.

**Impact** – This project helps reduce delays for airlines, lowers costs, and makes air travel safer by cutting down on human errors and workload. It also supports the safe use of automated flying technologies like autonomous taxiing and landing, which could change how planes operate in the future. By improving how flights are planned and controlled, ATMS makes the skies safer and more efficient for everyone.

**What's next step** – ATMS will continue testing and improving its technologies and safety methods. These will gradually be put into use to modernize air traffic control systems across the country. This ongoing work will help handle growing air traffic and support new flying technologies safely.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The ATMS project is creating smarter, safer air traffic management that prepares the skies for more flights and new flying technologies while cutting delays and costs.
articles.personality_title_fr: Un nouveau projet de gestion du trafic aérien pour rendre les vols plus sûrs et fluides
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Le système national de l’espace aérien (NAS) devient de plus en plus chargé et complexe avec la multiplication des types d’aéronefs. Gérer ce trafic en toute sécurité et efficacité est un grand défi.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Le projet Gestion et Sécurité du Trafic Aérien (ATMS) développe de nouvelles technologies et règles pour changer la façon dont le trafic aérien est contrôlé. Plutôt que de réagir aux problèmes au fur et à mesure (tactique), ATMS mise sur la planification à l’avance (stratégique) pour mieux gérer les vols. Il travaille en étroite collaboration avec la FAA pour créer des outils d’automatisation et des systèmes de sécurité qui permettent aux avions traditionnels et aux nouveaux types d’aéronefs de voler ensemble en toute sécurité.

**Impact** – Ce projet aide à réduire les retards des compagnies aériennes, baisse les coûts et rend les voyages aériens plus sûrs en réduisant les erreurs humaines et la charge de travail. Il soutient aussi l’utilisation sécurisée des technologies de vol automatisées comme le taxi autonome et l’atterrissage, qui pourraient transformer le fonctionnement des avions à l’avenir. En améliorant la planification et le contrôle des vols, ATMS rend le ciel plus sûr et plus efficace pour tous.

**Prochaine étape** – ATMS continuera à tester et améliorer ses technologies et méthodes de sécurité. Celles-ci seront progressivement mises en œuvre pour moderniser les systèmes de contrôle du trafic aérien à travers le pays. Ce travail permettra de gérer l’augmentation du trafic aérien et d’intégrer en toute sécurité les nouvelles technologies de vol.

**Conclusion en une phrase** – Le projet ATMS crée une gestion du trafic aérien plus intelligente et plus sûre, préparant le ciel à plus de vols et aux nouvelles technologies tout en réduisant retards et coûts.
articles.personality_title_es: Nuevo proyecto de gestión del tráfico aéreo para vuelos más seguros y fluidos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – El Sistema Nacional del Espacio Aéreo (NAS) se está volviendo más ocupado y complejo con la llegada de más tipos de aeronaves. Controlar todo este tráfico aéreo de forma segura y eficiente es un gran reto.

**Qué pasó** – El proyecto Gestión y Seguridad del Tráfico Aéreo (ATMS) está desarrollando nuevas tecnologías y reglas para cambiar cómo se controla el tráfico aéreo. En lugar de reaccionar a los problemas a medida que ocurren (táctico), ATMS se centra en planificar con anticipación (estratégico) para manejar mejor los vuelos. Trabaja en estrecha colaboración con la Administración Federal de Aviación (FAA) para crear herramientas de automatización y sistemas de seguridad que ayuden a que los aviones tradicionales y los nuevos tipos de vehículos aéreos vuelen juntos de forma segura.

**Impacto** – Este proyecto ayuda a reducir los retrasos de las aerolíneas, bajar costos y hacer que viajar en avión sea más seguro al disminuir errores humanos y la carga de trabajo. También apoya el uso seguro de tecnologías de vuelo automatizadas como el taxi autónomo y el aterrizaje, que podrían cambiar cómo operan los aviones en el futuro. Al mejorar la planificación y control de vuelos, ATMS hace que los cielos sean más seguros y eficientes para todos.

**Próximo paso** – ATMS continuará probando y mejorando sus tecnologías y métodos de seguridad. Estas se irán implementando poco a poco para modernizar los sistemas de control del tráfico aéreo en todo el país. Este trabajo ayudará a manejar el aumento del tráfico aéreo y a apoyar nuevas tecnologías de vuelo con seguridad.

**Frase clave** – El proyecto ATMS está creando una gestión del tráfico aéreo más inteligente y segura que prepara el cielo para más vuelos y nuevas tecnologías, al tiempo que reduce retrasos y costos.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a vast, gently glowing sky filled with a harmonious network of softly illuminated flight paths and diverse stylized aircraft silhouettes—traditional planes and futuristic autonomous drones—moving smoothly and safely along coordinated trajectories, symbolizing strategic air traffic flow and seamless integration in a calm, expansive airspace.

articles.title: About Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders Project
articles.summary: The Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders (AAMP) project accelerates advanced air mobility technologies for wildfire response and urban transportation through real-world demonstrations and strategic partnerships. AAMP researches emerging technologies, establishes aircraft strategic deconfliction frameworks, and validates solutions in metropolitan areas to enable larger-scale urban air mobility. The project enhances Unmanned Aircraft Systems capabilities for wildfire mitigation and disaster response by transferring Portable Airspace Management System technologies to enable routine, safe, […]
articles.content:
NASA / Kyle Jenkins

The Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders (AAMP) project accelerates advanced air mobility technologies for wildfire response and urban transportation through real-world demonstrations and strategic partnerships.

AAMP researches emerging technologies, establishes aircraft strategic deconfliction frameworks, and validates solutions in metropolitan areas to enable larger-scale urban air mobility.

The project enhances Unmanned Aircraft Systems capabilities for wildfire mitigation and disaster response by transferring Portable Airspace Management System technologies to enable routine, safe, and efficient Beyond Visual Line of Sight operations.

AAMP delivers scalable technologies, integration standards, and coordination tools that drive industry adoption and improve multi-agency collaboration for emergency response.

More AAMP details

The project is dedicated to demonstrating and validating the safe and practical integration of advanced air mobility technologies. We focus on developing, evaluating, and transferring performance requirements for:

Portable Airspace Management System — To enable safe, scalable, and continuous (24/7) aerial operations, especially in challenging degraded visual environments.

Airspace Service Providers — Managing medium-density advanced air mobility operations, aligning with the AAM National Strategy.

Our goal is to ensure these systems are ready for real-world use in emergency operations as well as urban transportation. AAMP actively collaborates with government agencies, academia, and industry stakeholders. These partnerships are vital for validating the safe and effective performance of these new technologies.

This, in turn, enables safe, practical, and resilient urban air mobility operations. AAMP bridges the gap between research and implementation, making Advanced Air Mobility a trusted solution for everyday transportation and life-saving missions across the United States.
articles.reasoning: The AAMP project represents a significant technological advancement with broad societal benefits by improving wildfire response and urban transportation through validated, scalable air mobility solutions. It involves real-world demonstrations and multi-agency collaboration, enhancing emergency response capabilities and enabling safe urban air mobility, which has meaningful positive impact on public safety and transportation infrastructure.
articles.personality_title: NASA’s Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders improve wildfire response and city transport
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Wildfires and busy city traffic are big problems that need new solutions. NASA’s Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders (AAMP) project works on new flying technologies to help with emergency responses and everyday urban travel.

**What happened** – The AAMP project is testing and improving drones and other unmanned aircraft to fight wildfires and support city transportation. They use real flights and work with different groups like government agencies and industry experts to make sure these technologies are safe and useful.

**Impact** – AAMP helps make flying vehicles safer and easier to use in crowded skies, especially during emergencies like wildfires. By improving how these aircraft are managed and coordinated, the project makes it possible to use drones beyond the pilot’s sight, which can save time and lives.

**What’s next step** – The project will continue to test and share these technologies so they can be used regularly in cities and emergency situations. This work will help more agencies adopt these tools and improve how they respond to disasters and move people around cities.

**One-sentence takeaway** – NASA’s AAMP project is bringing safer, smarter flying technology closer to everyday use for wildfire help and city travel.
articles.personality_title_fr: Le projet Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders de la NASA améliore la lutte contre les incendies et le transport urbain
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les incendies de forêt et la circulation dense en ville posent de grands problèmes. Le projet Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders (AAMP) de la NASA développe de nouvelles technologies de vol pour aider lors des urgences et dans les déplacements urbains quotidiens.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Le projet AAMP teste et améliore des drones et autres aéronefs sans pilote pour combattre les incendies et soutenir le transport en ville. Ils réalisent des vols réels et collaborent avec des agences gouvernementales et des experts pour assurer la sécurité et l’efficacité de ces technologies.

**Impact** – AAMP rend les véhicules volants plus sûrs et plus faciles à utiliser dans un ciel chargé, surtout en cas d’urgence comme les incendies. En améliorant la gestion et la coordination de ces aéronefs, le projet permet de piloter au-delà de la ligne de vue, ce qui peut sauver du temps et des vies.

**Prochaine étape** – Le projet continuera à tester et diffuser ces technologies pour une utilisation régulière en ville et en situations d’urgence. Cela aidera davantage d’agences à adopter ces outils et à améliorer la réponse aux catastrophes et les déplacements urbains.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Le projet AAMP de la NASA rapproche des technologies de vol plus sûres et plus intelligentes pour aider en cas d’incendies et dans les transports urbains.
articles.personality_title_es: El proyecto Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders de la NASA mejora la respuesta a incendios y el transporte urbano
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los incendios forestales y el tráfico en las ciudades son grandes problemas que necesitan nuevas soluciones. El proyecto Advanced Air Mobility Pathfinders (AAMP) de la NASA trabaja en nuevas tecnologías de vuelo para ayudar en emergencias y en el transporte diario urbano.

**Qué pasó** – El proyecto AAMP está probando y mejorando drones y otras aeronaves no tripuladas para combatir incendios y apoyar el transporte en las ciudades. Usan vuelos reales y colaboran con agencias gubernamentales y expertos para asegurarse de que estas tecnologías sean seguras y útiles.

**Impacto** – AAMP ayuda a que los vehículos voladores sean más seguros y fáciles de usar en cielos concurridos, especialmente durante emergencias como incendios. Al mejorar cómo se gestionan y coordinan estas aeronaves, el proyecto permite vuelos más allá de la vista del piloto, lo que puede ahorrar tiempo y vidas.

**Próximo paso** – El proyecto seguirá probando y compartiendo estas tecnologías para que se usen regularmente en ciudades y situaciones de emergencia. Esto ayudará a que más agencias adopten estas herramientas y mejoren la respuesta ante desastres y el transporte urbano.

**Frase clave** – El proyecto AAMP de la NASA acerca tecnologías de vuelo más seguras e inteligentes para ayudar en incendios y en el transporte urbano.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm painting of a futuristic city skyline at dusk with sleek, silent unmanned aerial vehicles gracefully navigating organized air corridors above, while below, coordinated emergency response teams symbolized by interconnected glowing pathways and softly illuminated control panels work seamlessly together, all rendered in natural, muted earth tones and soft blues.

articles.title: Möbius strip-like molecule has an entirely new and bizarre shape
articles.summary: A ring of 13 carbon atoms and two chlorine atoms has a remarkable molecular structure that means you would have to go around the loop four times to return to your starting position
articles.content:
A ring of 13 carbon atoms and two chlorine atoms has a remarkable molecular structure that means you would have to go around the loop four times to return to your starting position

Representation of the electrons in the “half-Möbius”-shaped molecule IBM Research and the University of Manchester

Chemists have discovered a new molecular shape, and it is twice as odd as the twisty Möbius strip.

The Möbius strip is a looped band with a twist, such that something tiny, such as an ant, would have to go around the loop twice to return to where it started on the same side of the strip.

Advertisement

Igor Rončević at the University of Manchester in the UK and his colleagues now discovered a molecule with an even stranger “half-Möbius” shape. Their experiment may be the first step towards a new way to engineer useful molecules by tuning their 3D shapes, or topology.

“This molecule is very new and very unexpected. The appeal is not just that we made a molecule with an unusual topology, but we also showed that this topology is possible, and no one really thought about it,” he says.

To make the molecule, the researchers used 13 carbon atoms and two chlorine atoms assembled into a ring-like shape on a thin surface of gold at an extremely cold temperature. They used two specialised microscopes – an atomic force microscope and a scanning tunnelling microscope – to control the atoms and map the properties of their electrons. In this type of molecule, the electrons aren’t tightly bound to their atoms; instead, the electrons spread across specific regions around the atoms like tiny waves of matter.

Free newsletter Sign up to The Daily The latest on what’s new in science and why it matters each day. Sign up to newsletter

It was the interactions between these electrons that produced the never-before-seen twistiness in the molecule. If a tiny quantum creature travelled along the atoms, it would take it four circuits of the ring to return to its starting point.

By prodding the molecule with a small electromagnetic pulse, the team was able to switch the molecule’s twist from left-handed to right-handed or to untwist it. The researchers could engineer its topology on demand, creating another way for chemists to manipulate molecules.

To understand the new molecule and why it could even exist, the team used simulations on both a conventional computer and an IBM quantum computer. Interactions between electrons were crucial for the molecule’s novel twists, and they are difficult to exactly simulate with conventional computers. But quantum computers are already built from interacting quantum objects, so they can perform simulations at a higher level of confidence, says Rončević.

This is an example of how quantum computers can already be useful for real-world chemistry problems, says team member Ivano Tavernelli at IBM.

“This experiment is a remarkable achievement across a number of dimensions: organic chemistry, surface science, nanoscience and quantum chemistry,” says Gemma Solomon at the University of Copenhagen in Denmark.

“This is a beautiful and inspiring study that brings abstract topological concepts vividly into the realm of molecular chemistry,” says Kenichiro Itami at the Japanese scientific institute RIKEN. He says the study is a technical tour de force.

Dongho Kim at Yonsei University in South Korea, a pioneer of past work on Möbius-like molecules, says being able to switch the molecule from one shape to another is particularly interesting, as it could lead to applications in sensors. For instance, molecules could switch in a pre-programmed way when exposed to magnetic fields.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant scientific breakthrough in molecular chemistry with the discovery of a novel molecule having a unique 'half-Möbius' topology. This discovery advances understanding of molecular shapes and electron behavior and demonstrates practical quantum computer applications in chemistry. The ability to engineer and switch molecular topology on demand has broad implications for future molecular engineering and sensor technologies, representing a meaningful and inspiring technological innovation with potential wide-reaching benefits.
articles.personality_title: Scientists create molecule with a new, unusual twist
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Scientists have long studied the Möbius strip, a loop with a twist that makes you travel twice around to return to the start. This shape inspired researchers to explore new molecular shapes with unusual twists.

**What happened** – Researchers at the University of Manchester and IBM created a tiny molecule made of 13 carbon atoms and 2 chlorine atoms arranged in a ring. This molecule has a unique “half-Möbius” shape, meaning you must go around it four times to return to your starting point. Using special microscopes and very cold temperatures, they controlled the atoms and studied how electrons move in this strange shape. They even switched the molecule’s twist from left to right or untwisted it using small electric pulses.

**Impact** – This discovery is important because it shows a new way molecules can be shaped and controlled. It could help scientists design molecules with specific properties, such as sensors that change when exposed to magnetic fields. Also, the research used quantum computers to better understand the molecule’s complex electron behavior, showing how this new technology can help in real chemistry problems.

**What's next step** – The researchers plan to explore more molecules with different shapes and how to control them. This could lead to new materials or devices that work in special ways, like smart sensors or advanced electronics.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Scientists have made a molecule with a never-before-seen twisty shape that can be switched on demand, opening new paths for molecular design and quantum computing in chemistry.
articles.personality_title_fr: Des scientifiques créent une molécule avec une torsion nouvelle et inhabituelle
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les scientifiques étudient depuis longtemps la bande de Möbius, une boucle avec une torsion qui oblige à faire deux tours pour revenir au point de départ. Cette forme a inspiré des chercheurs à explorer de nouvelles formes moléculaires avec des torsions inhabituelles.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs de l’Université de Manchester et d’IBM ont créé une toute petite molécule composée de 13 atomes de carbone et 2 atomes de chlore formant un anneau. Cette molécule possède une forme unique appelée « demi-Möbius », ce qui signifie qu’il faut faire quatre tours pour revenir au point de départ. Grâce à des microscopes spéciaux et des températures très basses, ils ont contrôlé les atomes et étudié comment les électrons se déplacent dans cette forme étrange. Ils ont même pu changer la torsion de la molécule de gauche à droite ou la détordre en utilisant de petites impulsions électriques.

**Impact** – Cette découverte est importante car elle montre une nouvelle façon de façonner et de contrôler les molécules. Cela pourrait aider les scientifiques à concevoir des molécules avec des propriétés spécifiques, comme des capteurs qui changent sous l’effet de champs magnétiques. De plus, la recherche a utilisé des ordinateurs quantiques pour mieux comprendre le comportement complexe des électrons, montrant comment cette nouvelle technologie peut aider en chimie réelle.

**Prochaine étape** – Les chercheurs prévoient d’explorer d’autres molécules avec différentes formes et comment les contrôler. Cela pourrait conduire à de nouveaux matériaux ou dispositifs fonctionnant de manière spéciale, comme des capteurs intelligents ou de l’électronique avancée.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Des scientifiques ont créé une molécule avec une forme torsadée jamais vue auparavant, pouvant être modifiée à volonté, ouvrant de nouvelles voies pour la conception moléculaire et l’informatique quantique en chimie.
articles.personality_title_es: Científicos crean una molécula con una torsión nueva y extraña
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los científicos han estudiado durante mucho tiempo la banda de Möbius, un lazo con una torsión que obliga a dar dos vueltas para regresar al punto de inicio. Esta forma inspiró a los investigadores a explorar nuevas formas moleculares con torsiones inusuales.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de la Universidad de Manchester y IBM crearon una molécula diminuta compuesta por 13 átomos de carbono y 2 átomos de cloro organizados en un anillo. Esta molécula tiene una forma única llamada “medio Möbius”, lo que significa que hay que dar cuatro vueltas para regresar al punto de partida. Usando microscopios especiales y temperaturas muy bajas, controlaron los átomos y estudiaron cómo se mueven los electrones en esta forma extraña. Incluso pudieron cambiar la torsión de la molécula de izquierda a derecha o enderezarla usando pequeños pulsos eléctricos.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento es importante porque muestra una nueva forma en que las moléculas pueden ser moldeadas y controladas. Podría ayudar a los científicos a diseñar moléculas con propiedades específicas, como sensores que cambian cuando se exponen a campos magnéticos. Además, la investigación usó computadoras cuánticas para comprender mejor el comportamiento complejo de los electrones, mostrando cómo esta nueva tecnología puede ayudar en problemas reales de química.

**Próximo paso** – Los investigadores planean explorar más moléculas con diferentes formas y cómo controlarlas. Esto podría llevar a nuevos materiales o dispositivos que funcionen de maneras especiales, como sensores inteligentes o electrónica avanzada.

**Frase clave** – Científicos crearon una molécula con una forma torsionada nunca antes vista que puede cambiarse a voluntad, abriendo nuevos caminos para el diseño molecular y la computación cuántica en química.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a glowing, intricately twisted molecular ring composed of thirteen interconnected carbon spheres and two distinct chlorine spheres, floating gently above a smooth golden surface; delicate, wave-like streams of light represent electrons flowing around the ring in a complex, quadruple-loop path, with subtle hints of shifting twists symbolizing the molecule’s ability to change shape under gentle electromagnetic influence.
